# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

content.subject: Task 2 — Pre-PR `gh secret delete NPM_TOKEN` (AC 1, 4, 7)
content.description: DESTRUCTIVE: delete repo-level NPM_TOKEN secret. Capture pre/post `gh secret list` transcripts. Verify post-state via `--json name --jq` filter and REST API 404 check. Confirm release env secrets remain {total_count:0,secrets:[]}. Skip the delete command if AC 2(d) showed secret already absent. **REQUIRES USER CONFIRMATION before executing.**

content.description: Create branch feat/v1-cleanup-npm-token. Verify via grep (zero stale claims, exactly one OIDC-only sentence). Run `npm run quality` (all 13 subcommands green). Commit + push.

When code-review lands `6-3 → done`, also flip `epic-6 → done` in the same pass. If code-review skill's scope does not cover epic-level flips, Armel can manually edit the line or sweep it in a future retrospective per Epic 5 retro Commitment 15.

+- **Epic 6 retrospective remains `optional`.** No retrospective was run as part of this story. Armel can elect to run one (Epic 6 is lower-value retro territory than Epics 1-5 — three mechanical cleanup stories vs. design-decision work) or skip it. Per sprint-status legend, `optional` is a valid terminal state if no retro fires.

+- **Adversarial-review pre-flight completed at create-story time.** The spec's AC-2 recon (a)-(h) was ground-truth-verified on `origin/main` at `d531945` at Task 1 time; no spec-drift surprises surfaced. The only minor divergence: `wc -l docs/RELEASING.md` returned `633` pre-edit, not the spec's ~880 estimate — this is a cosmetic estimate error, not a correctness gate (AC 5(f) tolerance is ±5 on the delta from the net-line-count estimate `+4`; actual delta is `-1`, which places divergence at `-5` — right at the boundary, absorbed by paragraph-per-line markdown source style where in-line prose growth doesn't add lines). Recorded for Epic 6 retrospective (if run) as a "spec estimate vs. actual" data point.

+- **NOT-bundled items preserved as standing flags.** Epic 5 retro P1 (`rc` dist-tag at `1.0.0-rc.3`), P2 (audit-evidence hardening before GH 90d retention), P3 (NFR9 install-smoke automation), P7 (`package.json.main` side-effectful entrypoint), issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility), Commitment 16 (`prevent_self_review: false → true` when second reviewer onboards) — all remain tracked, none bundled. **v1.0.0 is LIVE on npm under `--tag latest`** as of 2026-04-23T18:56:39Z (Story 5.3 dispatch run `24852899833`, PR #214 `c0b2b67` merged). Published via OIDC trusted publishing with SLSA L2 provenance. NFR6 immutability policy is ACTIVE — `v1.0.0` is forever-burned; rollback from this instant is `npm deprecate` + ship-forward only. `latest` flipped `0.10.0` → `1.0.0`; `rc` unchanged at `1.0.0-rc.3`; `alpha` unchanged.

**v1.0.0 is VERIFIED cross-platform** as of Story 5.4 (PRs #219 `075dbcb` + #220 `a6ba417` + #221 `4e7405a` review patches). `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch run `24856988815` PASSED all 3 matrix legs (`ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, `macos-latest`) — each printed `1.0.0` via `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@latest --version`, exited 0, total wall-clock 46s. NFR9 elapsed-time `01:27` (publish → smoke completion) — missed 60-min target by 27 min, classified `missed-target-non-blocking` (zero live defects surfaced). **Epic 5 CLOSED 2026-04-24** after second-pass code-review flipped 5-4 `review → done` (2 MEDIUM patches deferred to Epic 6: VERSION input allowlist + OUTPUT identity assertion in `install-smoke.yaml`; node-version drift decision resolved as dismiss). Epic 6 cleanup (6-1 delete legacy workflows, 6-2 remove `release:*` scripts, 6-3 remove `NPM_TOKEN`) is now unblocked.

**Why:** Story 5.2 (rc.3 cut) surfaced 5 pipeline defects over 7 attempts / ~6h; Story 5.3 dispatched on first attempt (7m 1s end-to-end, well within NFR1's 20-min target) as evidence those fixes held. Story 5.4 shipped on first attempt as well (two-commit envelope, single smoke-workflow dispatch, zero retries). Story 5.2's rc.1 and rc.2 release commits remain on `main` as cosmetic stales (never tagged, never published) — noted in the launch audit's Derogation section.

**How to apply:**

- When asked about v1.0.0 status, `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags` is authoritative: `latest: "1.0.0"` confirms Story 5.3 landed. `rc: "1.0.0-rc.3"` will remain pinned unless a future rc is cut.
- Story 5.4 verification evidence lives at `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification` (6 subsections: workflow run, matrix table, NFR9 elapsed-time record, marketplace.json invariant, observations, decision). Load that for forensic replay of the cross-platform verification.
- `install-smoke.yaml` is now a **permanent reusable artifact** — every future release (v1.0.1, v1.1.0, v2.0.0, ...) redispatches with `gh workflow run install-smoke.yaml -f version=<dist-tag-or-version> --ref main` to produce identical 3-leg cross-platform evidence. Keeps NFR9 coverage cheap for every subsequent cut.
- NFR1 rolling-10 series: `[Story 5.2 rc.3: 5m 4s, Story 5.3 v1.0.0: 7m 1s]` — continues to fill across subsequent cuts (5.4 is verification-only, no release.yaml dispatch; next NFR1 data point comes from v1.0.1 / v1.1.0 / etc.).
- NFR9 data point 1: `missed-target-non-blocking` at `01:27` (Story 5.4). Epic 5 retrospective will weigh whether to (i) keep 60-min + automate via tag-push trigger, (ii) soften to 24h for solo-maintainer, or (iii) require on-call commitment.
- v1.0.0 tag is an **annotated tag object** — `git rev-parse v1.0.0` returns the tag SHA, not the commit; use `git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}'` to dereference to the commit SHA when verifying tag anchors.
- For provenance verification, use `npm view @<version> --json | jq '.dist'` (or `jq '.dist.attestations'`) — NOT the `jq '{version, dist: {tarball, shasum, attestations}}'` shorthand (which returns nulls because those fields live inside `.dist`, not at top level).

Story 6.3 dev-story COMPLETE. Here's the summary:

## What shipped

- **PR #224** merged as `45bed3a` at `2026-04-24T19:32:05Z` — admin-bypass squash merge, 7/7 required checks PASS, pre-commit/commit-msg hooks passed without `--no-verify`.
- **Pre-PR state change:** `gh secret delete NPM_TOKEN --repo armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge` — verified absent via three independent API surfaces (`list` empty, `--jq length` returns `0`, REST endpoint returns `404`).
- **One file modified:** `docs/RELEASING.md` (net `-1` line) — four AC-5 prose reconciliations in one pass:
  - `:85` Release Environment: transitional → terminal framing + broadened invariant
  - `:177` npm Trusted Publisher: legacy-residual → OIDC-only (embeds Epic 6 close-out sentence as permanent doc home)
  - `:426-427` Rollback Playbook: two token-compromise bullets consolidated to one exception-path bullet
  - `:80` + `:157` reviewed, no edit
- **Epic 6 close-out sentence** now lives at `docs/RELEASING.md:177`: *"SKF's npm publish credential surface is now OIDC-only. Future incidents requiring credential revocation involve npmjs.com's trusted-publisher config, not a repo secret."*
- **Release-env invariant upheld:** `gh api .../environments/release/secrets` still `{"total_count":0,"secrets":[]}` (Story 1.2 contract unchanged — separate API surfaces from repo-level secret).
- **NFR6 immutability confirmed:** `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@latest` still `1.0.0`.

## Story file state

- Path: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/6-3-remove-npm-token-secret-from-repo-settings.md`
- Status: **`review`** (flipped from `ready-for-dev`)
- Sprint-status: `6-3-…: ready-for-dev → in-progress → review`; `epic-6: in-progress` (deliberately held — final flip pairs with `6-3 → done` via code-review)
- All 7 dev-story tasks marked `[x]` with subtasks; Dev Agent Record populated (Agent Model, Debug Log References, Completion Notes, File List); Change Log appended

## Next steps

Run `/bmad-code-review` on PR #224 in a fresh Claude Code session — **use a different LLM than Opus 4.7 for a genuine second opinion.** When code-review flips `6-3-…: review → done`, also flip `epic-6: in-progress → done` in the same pass to close the three-story Epic 6 cleanup arc atomically.

Any part of the implementation you'd like me to walk through — the four doc-prose edits, the `:426-427` consolidation rationale, the pre-PR sequencing logic, or something else?
